title: Benedict Burgh's Cato Paraphrase
summary: CME00106 is the checked-in copy of the University of Michigan encoding of Max Förster's eclectic Middle English edition, published in two separately paginated Archiv installments in 1905 and 1906. Förster prints a seven-stanza Parvus Cato from Cambridge University Library MS Hh.4.12 and a 161-stanza Cato Major based on Bodleian MS. Rawl. C. 48 but emended 236 times from a broad witness and early-print collation; his German glossary follows. The composite work model preserves the uncertain authorship of the Parvus, the four-book Burgh Cato Major, the Latin Disticha source, the physical manuscripts, editorial intermediaries, print editions, and digital surrogates as distinct entities. DIMEV v1.0 supplies a current collateral census and must not be read backward into Förster's source set. Complete public IIIF objects for Cambridge Ff.1.6, Durham Cosin V.ii.14, Göttingen 8 Cod. Ms. philol. 163 n, and NLW Peniarth 481 are cached as whole-object bundles with work loci; neither of Förster's two base witnesses has a complete public facsimile verified here.
review_notes: Förster's serial article is represented as one editorial project containing two separately citable installments.
The short Parvus Cato and long Cato Major have separate DIMEV records and separate attribution evidence; their consecutive printing does not make Burgh's authorship of both secure.
Napier's Rawlinson transcript and Greenwell's Durham collation are modeled provisionally as catalog descriptions because the schema has no dedicated editorial-intermediary entity type.
Modern DIMEV additions are collateral census witnesses unless a Förster relation is independently evidenced.

entities.label: General Corpus CME00106 XML artifact
entities.description: The exact XML file checked into the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME electronic text CME00106
entities.description: A manually keyed SGML/TEI-derived encoding of Förster's printed edition from volume 115 page 303 through volume 116 page 40, including the Middle English verse, notes, page breaks, and German glossary but omitting the first installment's pages 298–302 introduction.

entities.label: Middle English Parvus Cato and Benedict Burgh's Cato Major
entities.description: The composite discovery unit encoded by CME00106: a short Parvus Cato of disputed attribution followed by Burgh's much longer Cato Major. It is neither a physical witness nor Förster's reconstructed text.

entities.label: Parvus Cato
entities.description: The short Middle English component cataloged by DIMEV 6321 / NIMEV 3955. Förster prints seven stanzas from Hh.4.12 but doubts that Burgh composed or translated it.
notes: Burgh is a disputed attributed creator for this component, not a confirmed author.

entities.label: Cato Major
entities.description: Benedict Burgh's four-book, 161-stanza Middle English rhyme-royal adaptation of the Disticha Catonis, cataloged by DIMEV 1418 / NIMEV 854.

date_statements.label: composition or translation
date_statements.value: probably 1433–1440, for the young William Bourchier

entities.label: Disticha Catonis
entities.description: The late-antique Latin moral school text adapted in both Middle English components. Burgh's exact Latin exemplar is unidentified; Baehrens's 1881 edition is only Förster's modern numbering and comparison source.

entities.label: Max Förster's Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase editorial project
entities.description: One eclectic editorial project published in two Archiv installments. Förster chooses Hh.4.12 for Parvus and Rawl. C.48 for Major, collates the historically listed witness set and early prints directly or through supplied transcripts and collations, and provides an extensive German glossary.

bibliographic.citation: Max Förster, 'Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 115 (1905): 298–323; 116 (1906): 25–40.
bibliographic.title: Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

entities.label: Förster, Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase, Archiv 115 (1905)
entities.description: The first separately paginated installment: study on pp. 298–302 and the edition from Parvus stanza I through Cato Major stanza CXVIII on pp. 303–323.

bibliographic.citation: Max Förster, 'Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 115 (1905): 298–323.
bibliographic.title: Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

entities.label: Förster, Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase (Schluß), Archiv 116 (1906)
entities.description: The concluding separately paginated installment: Cato Major stanzas CXIX–CLXVIII on pp. 25–34 and Förster's German glossary on pp. 35–40.

bibliographic.citation: Max Förster, 'Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase (Schluß),' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 116 (1906): 25–40.
bibliographic.title: Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase (Schluß)

entities.reproduction_format: Complete 506-page PDF scan with OCR and metadata

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Archiv volume 116
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 508-page PDF scan with OCR and metadata

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records 6321 and 1418
entities.description: Versioned modern scholarly census for Parvus Cato and Cato Major, including manuscript and early-print witness records. It postdates Förster and is collateral evidence rather than a source he consulted.

entities.label: Baehrens, Poetae Latini Minores III, Disticha Catonis
entities.description: The modern Latin edition and numbering source cited by Förster in the margins. It is not the medieval exemplar used by Burgh.

bibliographic.citation: Emil Baehrens, ed., Poetae Latini Minores, vol. 3 (Leipzig: B. G. Teubner, 1881), 214–235.
bibliographic.title: Poetae Latini Minores

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Baehrens, Poetae Latini Minores III–IV
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 764-page combined-volume PDF scan with OCR and metadata

entities.label: William Caxton's Burgh Cato, STC 4851
entities.description: The earliest currently distinguished Caxton edition, circa 1476, represented by the Huntington copy R.B.62364. It is not STC 4850, the separate edition Förster collated.

entities.label: William Caxton's Burgh Cato, STC 4850
entities.description: A distinct circa-1477 Caxton edition surviving uniquely in Cambridge University Library AB.8.48.2. This is Förster's collated 'first Caxton' and Jenkinson's 1906 facsimile source.

entities.label: William Caxton's Burgh Cato, STC 4852
entities.description: A third distinct Caxton edition, circa 1483; it must not be conflated with either STC 4850 or STC 4851.

entities.label: William Copland's Isocrates and Burgh Cato, STC 14276
entities.description: The London 1557/1558 print collated by Förster. It reverses the medieval component order to Major then Parvus and omits the Parvus dedication.

entities.label: Francis Jenkinson's facsimile of Caxton STC 4850
entities.description: A complete 1906 printed facsimile of the unique Cambridge STC 4850 copy: 33 facsimile leaves and a blank, issued in 250 copies.

bibliographic.citation: Parvus Cato, Magnus Cato, printed by William Caxton, edited in facsimile by Francis Jenkinson (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1906).
bibliographic.title: Parvus Cato, Magnus Cato

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Jenkinson's Caxton facsimile
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 82-page PDF scan with OCR and metadata

entities.label: Fumio Kuriyagawa, Paruus Cato, Magnus Cato (1974)
entities.description: A later Caxton-based scholarly edition in Seijo English Monographs 13. It is a downstream comparison edition, not an input to Förster or CME00106.

bibliographic.citation: Fumio Kuriyagawa, ed., Paruus Cato, Magnus Cato, Seijo English Monographs 13 (Tokyo: Seijo University, 1974).
bibliographic.title: Paruus Cato, Magnus Cato

entities.label: A. Napier's transcript of Bodleian MS. Rawl. poet. 32
entities.description: A careful transcript supplied to Förster. It is an editorial intermediary, provisionally represented as a catalog description because the schema has no transcript type; its current survival and location were not established.

entities.label: William Greenwell's exact collation of Durham Cosin MS V.ii.14
entities.description: An exact collation supplied to Förster by Canon William Greenwell. It is an editorial intermediary, provisionally represented as a catalog description; its current survival and location were not established.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. e. 15
entities.description: Parvus ff. 5r–6v; Major ff. 6v–36v in the DIMEV census. A modern collateral census witness, not identified in Förster's historical sigla.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. D. 328
entities.description: Parvus ff. 161r–162r in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's source set.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. C. 48
entities.description: Förster's selected Cato Major base, ff. 84r–111v: a parchment English manuscript dated 1490–1500 in the current catalog. Förster calls it probably the oldest and least corrupt available copy but emends it 236 times.

notes: The public Digital Bodleian record exposes only one selected slide image, not a complete manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. poet. 32
entities.description: Cato Major at current ff. 2r–29v (Förster and DIMEV use a broader older locus). Förster worked from A. Napier's careful transcript.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. poet. 35
entities.description: Cato Major ff. 1r–17r; a late-fifteenth-century paper manuscript collated for Förster.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ee.4.31
entities.description: Parvus ff. 7r–7v and Major ff. 7v–24r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.1.6 (Findern Manuscript)
entities.description: Cato Major at old ff. 181r–185v, visually mapped in the current complete facsimile to ff. 155r–159v and canvases 313–322. Collated for Förster.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.4.9
entities.description: Parvus ff. 87v–88v and Major ff. 88v–107r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Hh.4.12
entities.description: Förster's selected Parvus Cato base. DIMEV records the Parvus at ff. 28v–30 and Cato Major at ff. 1r–28r, while Förster's printed milestones place the Parvus at his fols. 29v–31r. No complete public facsimile was verified to reconcile the foliation.

entities.label: Jesus College Cambridge MS 56
entities.description: Cato Major ff. 78v–92v. Förster knew the witness but explicitly had not seen it; its placement remained provisional.

entities.label: Magdalene College Cambridge Pepys MS 2006
entities.description: Parvus pp. 211–212 and Major pp. 213–224; collated for Förster.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS 1157
entities.description: Cato Major at f. 60 in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 29729
entities.description: Cato Major at f. 288v in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 34193
entities.description: Cato Major ff. 204r–223r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 38179
entities.description: Parvus ff. 20r–20v and Major from f. 21r in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: British Library Arundel MS 168
entities.description: Parvus f. 7r and Major ff. 7v–14r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 43
entities.description: Cato Major ff. 3r–4v in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 116
entities.description: Parvus ff. 98r–99r and Major ff. 99r–124r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 172
entities.description: Cato Major ff. 52r–71r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 271
entities.description: Parvus ff. 26r–26v and Major ff. 26v–44v; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2251
entities.description: Parvus ff. 169r–169v and Major ff. 170r–178r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4733
entities.description: Parvus ff. 3r–3v and Major ff. 4r–30r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 7333
entities.description: Parvus at f. 25r and Major ff. 25r–30v; collated for Förster.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 B xlvii
entities.description: Cato Major at f. 3v in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 D ii
entities.description: Cato Major ff. 207r–209r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: National Library of Wales Peniarth MS 481
entities.description: The current catalog explicitly identifies Peniarth 481 as former Ancient Peniarth 38, resolving Förster's P shelfmark. Parvus begins f. 1r and Major f. 2r; visual inspection finds the complete Cato ending at f. 27r. Förster had not seen P.

notes: The original manuscript is restricted, but its complete digital surrogate is public.
The original velvet-covered wooden boards survive.

entities.label: Durham University Library Cosin MS V.ii.14
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century manuscript with Parvus ff. 69r–70r and Major ff. 70r–92r. Förster used Canon Greenwell's exact collation rather than silently treating the digital object as his source.

entities.label: University of Glasgow Hunterian MS 259
entities.description: Parvus pp. 1–3 and Major pp. 3–50; collated for Förster.

entities.label: Chetham's Library MS 8009
entities.description: Parvus ff. 48r–49r and Major ff. 49v–75r; collated for Förster.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 3597
entities.description: Parvus ff. 89v–95r in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: Morgan Library & Museum MS M.775
entities.description: Parvus ff. 293r–294r and Major ff. 294r–320r in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: Huntington Library MS HM 144
entities.description: Parvus ff. 114r–114v and Major ff. 114v–135v; collated for Förster under its Huth designation.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin MS 519
entities.description: Cato Major ff. 2r–11v; collated for Förster.

entities.label: SUB Göttingen 8 Cod. Ms. philol. 163 n
entities.description: An English fifteenth-century codex. Visual inspection corrects the current work loci to Parvus ff. 1v–2r and Major ff. 2v–30r, corresponding to old ff. 44v–45r and 45v–72r; DIMEV's Parvus terminal '84' is a data error.

entities.label: Venerable English College Rome MS AVCAU 1405
entities.description: Parvus f. 89r and Major from f. 90r in the DIMEV census; collateral to Förster's documented source set.

entities.label: Raine manuscript Y
entities.description: A witness known to and collated for Förster, then associated with the Raine collection. Its present identity and location are unresolved. DIMEV's tentative association with Peniarth 481 conflicts with the independently confirmed identification of Peniarth 481 as Förster's separate P.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library complete IIIF facsimile of MS Ff.1.6
entities.description: A complete 329-canvas IIIF object. The Cato Major work portion is old ff. 181r–185v / current ff. 155r–159v, canvases 313–322.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and one 1200-pixel JPEG per canvas

entities.label: Durham complete IIIF facsimile of Cosin MS V.ii.14
entities.description: A complete 242-canvas IIIF object. Parvus and Major occupy ff. 69r–92r, canvases 147–193.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and one 1200-pixel JPEG per canvas

entities.label: SUB Göttingen complete IIIF facsimile of 8 Cod. Ms. philol. 163 n
entities.description: A complete 73-canvas IIIF object. Parvus occupies canvases 6–7 and Major canvases 8–63.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and one 1200-pixel JPEG per canvas

entities.label: National Library of Wales complete IIIF facsimile of Peniarth MS 481
entities.description: A complete 346-canvas IIIF object. The Cato occupies ff. 1r–27r, canvases 9–61; the remainder of the codex is retained in the bundle.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and one 1200-pixel JPEG per canvas

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected slide image for MS. Rawl. C. 48
entities.description: One selected public slide image, not a complete codex facsimile and not sufficient to inspect the complete Cato locus.

rights.component: Medieval Middle English textual content, excluding modern editions, encodings, catalog data, and images
rights.basis: The medieval text predates modern copyright terms by centuries; this does not determine rights in any modern representation.

rights.component: Medieval Middle English textual content, excluding modern editions, encodings, catalog data, and images
rights.basis: The fifteenth-century text predates modern copyright terms by centuries; this does not determine rights in any modern representation.

rights.component: Public U-M digital-text representation of the 1905–1906 Förster edition
rights.basis: The U-M item provider labels the item public domain and supplies the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement. This scoped provider assertion does not cover manuscript images or third-party catalogs.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files for Archiv volume 115
rights.basis: The Internet Archive metadata records NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the 1905 source volume. This provider assertion is scoped to the scanned publication and jurisdiction.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files for Archiv volume 116
rights.basis: The Internet Archive metadata records NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the 1906 source volume. This provider assertion is scoped to the scanned publication and jurisdiction.

rights.component: Cambridge Digital Library downloadable manuscript images and supplied metadata in the complete cached IIIF object
rights.basis: The Cambridge Digital Library attribution and metadata in the current object specify Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 3.0 for downloadable images and metadata.

rights.component: Durham manuscript images in the complete cached IIIF object
rights.basis: The current Durham IIIF object specifies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International.

rights.component: Current IIIF manifest and manuscript image services represented in the complete cached object
rights.basis: The current IIIF manifest declares the Public Domain Mark 1.0. A legacy per-page PDF wrapper displays restrictive noncommercial and no-repository-donation language, so downstream reuse should retain the conflict caveat and recheck current provider terms.
notes: The public-domain status asserted by the live IIIF manifest and the legacy wrapper terms are not silently reconciled.

rights.component: NLW IIIF manifest and manuscript images in the complete cached object

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files for the 1881 Baehrens volumes
rights.basis: The source publication is from 1881 and the Internet Archive makes the scan publicly downloadable; the claim is scoped to the historical publication and scan files.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files for Jenkinson's 1906 facsimile
rights.basis: The Internet Archive metadata records NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the 1906 facsimile volume. This does not by itself assign rights in the physical Cambridge copy or unrelated modern photographs.

rights.component: Any legacy British Library manuscript images, if service access is restored

editorial_practices.source_feature: Divergent manuscript transmission of Parvus and Major
editorial_practices.representation: Parvus printed from Hh.4.12; Major based on Rawl. C.48
editorial_practices.description: Förster selected different base witnesses for the two components, so the consecutive edition is not a transcript of one medieval codex.
editorial_practices.scope: Parvus stanzas I–VII and Cato Major stanzas VIII–CLXVIII

editorial_practices.source_feature: Variants across 25 historically listed manuscripts and four early prints
editorial_practices.representation: Eclectic Rawl. C.48-based Major text with 236 documented emendations and a variant apparatus
editorial_practices.description: All historically listed manuscripts except P and Q were available to Förster through texts, transcripts, or collations. Napier's transcript and Greenwell's exact collation are separately modeled intermediaries. Modern DIMEV additions are not retroactively treated as Förster sources.

editorial_practices.representation: Punctuation silently modernized and manuscript caesura points omitted
editorial_practices.description: Förster states that he silently modernizes punctuation and omits the points used to mark caesurae.
Förster regularizes capitalization instead of preserving each base witness's usage diplomatically.
Förster expands manuscript abbreviations and marks expansions typographically with italics.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Latin distichs that commonly precede English stanzas in witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Latin usually omitted; marginal Baehrens numbering retained for comparison
editorial_practices.description: Förster generally does not print the Latin source passages found with the English in manuscripts. His Baehrens references do not identify Burgh's exact exemplar.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English edition and marginal Latin-source references

editorial_practices.source_feature: Förster's full article across two serial installments
editorial_practices.representation: Volume 115 begins at printed p. 303; volume 116 pp. 25–40 is encoded in full
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding omits Förster's first-installment study on pp. 298–302 but retains the edited verse, notes, page breaks, and volume-116 glossary.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME submodule, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00106.xml.
evidence.summary: Direct structural inspection confirms all internal identifiers, U-M source title, encoded page sequence, four poem DIV2 divisions, 168 LG, 1,186 raw L, 82 glossary P, 37 PB, 253 NOTE, and 607 MILESTONE elements. The medieval verse count is 1,185 because one L contains the Parvus colophon rather than verse.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Middle English Compendium electronic text CME00106, 'Die Burghsche Cato-paraphrase.'
evidence.summary: Confirms the current handle, title, Förster responsibility, Braunschweig/Westermann 1905–1906 publication statement, and U-M public-domain / No Copyright–United States provider statements.

evidence.citation: Max Förster, 'Die Burghsche Cato-Paraphrase,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 115 (1905): 298–323; 116 (1906): 25–40.
evidence.locator: 115:298–323; 116:25–40; especially 115:298–303 for attribution, date, witness census, bases, and method; 116:35–40 glossary
evidence.summary: Direct page audit supports the two-installment boundary, probable 1433–1440 date and William Bourchier context, twenty-five-manuscript/four-print historical census, base witnesses, uncertain Parvus attribution, Napier transcript, Greenwell collation, P and Q not seen, editorial normalizations, 236 Rawl. C.48 emendations, stanza sequence, glossary, and Baehrens comparison citations.
notes: The first installment stops at stanza CXVIII; the second begins at CXIX.
Förster's old print labels are reconciled with modern STC distinctions only where supported by modern bibliography.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan of Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 115 (1905), University of Toronto copy.
evidence.locator: Printed pp. 298–323 = PDF pages 310–335; U-M encoded portion begins printed p. 303 = PDF page 315
evidence.summary: Complete 506-page volume scan inspected for title-page publication data, article extent, encoded-source boundary, and physical page mapping. PDF, metadata, and OCR derivatives are cached separately with recorded hashes.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan of Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 116 (1906), University of Toronto copy.
evidence.summary: Complete 508-page volume scan inspected for title-page publication data, concluding article extent, stanza continuation, and glossary. PDF, metadata, and OCR derivatives are cached separately with recorded hashes.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0 (2025-01-01), records 6321 and 1418 and associated witness data, Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14585961.
evidence.locator: records 6321 and 1418; manuscript and printed-book witness entries
evidence.summary: The complete versioned release supports the separate Parvus and Major records, NIMEV numbers, normalized incipits, verse forms, subjects, current witness census, loci, and early-print identifiers. The record-6321 prose description's 1,185-line count conflicts with its own short component loci and is not used for the Parvus extent. Modern additions remain collateral to Förster.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, current Medieval Manuscripts catalog records for MSS. Rawl. C. 48, Rawl. poet. 32, and Rawl. poet. 35; underlying TEI catalog data reviewed at repository commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmarks, catalog identifiers, dates, materials, Cato contents, and that the public digital records expose only selected slide images rather than complete Rawlinson codices.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Digital Library, MS Ff.1.6 (Findern Manuscript), complete 329-canvas IIIF object.
evidence.summary: Manifest, metadata, rights labels, canvas inventory, and page images were audited. Visual inspection reconciles Förster/DIMEV's old Cato locus with the current foliation. The whole 329-canvas object is cached, not only the work pages.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Cosin MS V.ii.14 catalog record.
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, mid-fifteenth-century manuscript description, Parvus and Major items and loci, Burgh attribution, probable 1433–1440 date, and witness-specific omissions and variants.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, complete IIIF facsimile of Cosin MS V.ii.14, ARK ark:/32150/t2mjm214p80v.
evidence.summary: The live 242-canvas manifest, rights label, complete inventory, and relevant pages were audited; the complete object is cached with one image per canvas.

evidence.citation: Göttingen State and University Library, 8 Cod. Ms. philol. 163 n, complete 73-canvas IIIF object DE-611-HS-3558283.
evidence.locator: Parvus current ff. 1v–2r, canvases 6–7; Major current ff. 2v–30r, canvases 8–63; old loci ff. 44v–72r
evidence.summary: Manifest, current Public Domain Mark, canvas inventory, and page images were audited. Visual inspection corrects DIMEV's Parvus terminal '84' and maps the refoliated work. The legacy per-page PDF wrapper's restrictive wording conflicts with the live IIIF rights mark and is preserved as a caveat.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Peniarth MS 481, Disticha Catonis catalog record.
evidence.summary: Confirms that Peniarth 481 was formerly Ancient Peniarth MS 38, identifying it with Förster's separate siglum P; records vellum support, 170 leaves, dimensions, original binding, and restricted original access. This makes DIMEV's tentative identification of Raine Y with the same manuscript problematic rather than resolved.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, complete IIIF facsimile of Peniarth MS 481, digital object 4393138.
evidence.locator: Parvus starts f. 1r; Major starts f. 2r; Cato explicit f. 27r; canvases 9–61
evidence.summary: The 346-canvas manifest, Public Domain Mark, complete inventory, and page images were audited. Visual inspection establishes the whole Cato boundary: f. 27v–29r is blank and another work begins with its frontispiece at f. 29v. The complete codex is cached.

evidence.citation: British Library Explore Archives and Manuscripts current records for the Förster-siglum British Library witnesses; service-status check for legacy digitized manuscript images.
evidence.summary: Confirms current catalog endpoints for Harley 116, 172, 271, 2251, 4733, 7333, Arundel 168, Add 34193, and the Royal 18 D ii Cato component. The legacy image service remained unavailable following the cyberattack; the manifests therefore say unavailable, not never digitized.

evidence.citation: DIMEV v1.0 printed-book witness records for Cato Major and Parvus Cato, checked against Francis Jenkinson's 1906 facsimile description of the unique Cambridge Caxton copy.
evidence.summary: Supports separation of STC 4851 (circa 1476), STC 4850 (circa 1477, unique CUL AB.8.48.2), and STC 4852 (circa 1483); identifies STC 4850 as Jenkinson's source and distinguishes the Burgh verse prints from unrelated prose Caton STC 4853. Förster's historical 'first Caxton' is mapped to STC 4850, not silently equated with the earliest currently distinguished edition.

evidence.citation: Francis Jenkinson, ed., Parvus Cato, Magnus Cato, printed by William Caxton (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1906), Internet Archive item parvuscatomagnus00burguoft.
evidence.locator: Title and limitation matter; 33 facsimile leaves and blank; complete 82-page scan
evidence.summary: Direct inspection confirms the unique Cambridge source copy, facsimile extent, 250-copy limitation, bibliographic statement, and complete scan. PDF, metadata, and OCR derivatives are cached separately.

evidence.citation: Emil Baehrens, ed., Poetae Latini Minores, vols. 3–4, Internet Archive item poetaelatinimino34baeh; Disticha Catonis at vol. 3, pp. 214–235.
evidence.summary: Direct audit confirms the exact Latin edition, date, page span, and PDF mapping cited by Förster. Its relationship is modern scholarly comparison and numbering, not medieval exemplar.

evidence.citation: Fumio Kuriyagawa, ed., Paruus Cato, Magnus Cato, Seijo English Monographs 13 (Tokyo: Seijo University, 1974), bibliographic record and cited-edition descriptions.
evidence.summary: Supports the existence, title, editor, series, date, and Caxton-based framing of this later comparison edition. The full edition was not locally cached or treated as a source for CME00106.

evidence.citation: Current Bodleian and Cambridge University Library catalog, digital-collection, and reproduction-service search for MSS. Rawl. C. 48 and Hh.4.12.
evidence.summary: No complete public color facsimile was verified for either base witness. Rawl. C.48 has one selected public slide image; Hh.4.12 has no complete public image object found. Both repositories provide request routes, so the status is no public source found rather than nondigitized.

evidence.citation: Local integrity audit of CME00106 source-cache objects, including four complete IIIF bundles and historical-edition downloads.
evidence.summary: Recorded SHA-256 and byte sizes were recomputed; PDF structure, ZIP integrity, tar/gzip integrity, embedded IIIF manifest and inventory presence, manifest canvas counts, image counts, nonzero files, and JPEG decodability were checked. Each new cache object is hard-linked to the corresponding path in the main checkout.
notes: This evidence record points to one representative bundle; every local copy is independently enumerated with its own path, byte size, and hash in access.local_copies.

open_questions.question: Was the Parvus Cato composed or translated by Benedict Burgh, or independently appended to the Cato Major tradition?
next_steps: Consult a modern critical study or edition devoted to DIMEV 6321.
Compare complete Parvus witnesses and their Latin framing without treating Förster's Hh.4.12 base as an authorial original.

open_questions.question: What is the present identity and location of Förster's Raine manuscript Y?
next_steps: Trace Raine-collection ownership and sale records after Förster.
Do not adopt DIMEV's tentative Peniarth 481 association unless the conflict with Peniarth 481's confirmed former identity as Ancient Peniarth 38, Förster's separate P, is resolved.

open_questions.question: What current stemma and critical text best refine or supersede Förster's reconstruction across the enlarged DIMEV witness census?
next_steps: Inspect Kuriyagawa's 1974 edition and later textual studies in full.
Keep DIMEV-only additions collateral unless a source explicitly demonstrates Förster's knowledge or use of them.

open_questions.question: Can complete color facsimiles be obtained for Förster's Hh.4.12 Parvus base and Rawl. C.48 Major base?
next_steps: Request whole-codex reproductions from Cambridge University Library and the Bodleian, including covers, boards, pastedowns, flyleaves, every folio, blanks, stubs, and edges where feasible.
Record delivered-file terms separately, map Rawl. C.48 ff. 84r–111v, and reconcile DIMEV's Hh.4.12 loci with Förster's one-folio-later Parvus milestones inside the complete objects.

open_questions.question: Do A. Napier's Rawl. poet. 32 transcript and William Greenwell's Durham collation survive, and where are they held?
next_steps: Search Förster's papers and correspondence for the supplied transcript and collation.
If found, replace the provisional catalog-description modeling with a dedicated intermediary type when the lineage schema supports one.
notes: Their historical use is secure from Förster's statement; only present survival and location are unknown.
